Title: miR2105 and the kinase OsSAPK10 co-regulate OsbZIP86 to mediate drought-induced ABA biosynthesis in rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: miR2105 (osa-miR2105) is a 20-nt drought-, ABA-, and salt-repressed miRNA in rice. Its downregulation allows stress-induced upregulation of OsbZIP86, whereas miR2105 overexpression prevents OsbZIP86 induction under stress. Overexpression of OsbZIP86 can partially rescue OsNCED3 expression in miR2105-ox protoplasts, supporting that miR2105 acts mainly through OsbZIP86 to affect OsNCED3 and ABA biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. miR2105 expression is repressed by ABA, drought, and salt, whereas its target OsbZIP86 is induced under these conditions.
2. miR2105 directly cleaves OsbZIP86 mRNA at the complementary site, as shown by 5'-RLM-RACE.
3. miR2105 overexpression decreases, and STTM2105-mediated knockdown increases, drought and salt tolerance, affects stomatal aperture, and oppositely alters ABA levels under drought.
4. Changes in OsNCED3 transcription and ABA content in miR2105 transgenic lines demonstrate that miR2105 negatively regulates drought-induced ABA biosynthesis via OsbZIP86.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of drought- and salt-induced ABA biosynthesis and stress tolerance by directing cleavage of OsbZIP86 mRNA; overexpression reduces ABA accumulation, drought and salt tolerance, and stomatal closure under stress, while knockdown has opposite effects.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: miR2105 cleaves OsbZIP86 mRNA at the complementary site, lowering OsbZIP86 transcript levels and thereby indirectly reducing OsNCED3 expression and ABA biosynthesis under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in an ABA biosynthesis and signaling module under drought and salt stress by negatively regulating the OsSAPK10–OsbZIP86–OsNCED3 pathway that controls drought-induced ABA accumulation and stomatal closure.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: miR2105 --(miRNA-directed mRNA cleavage inhibition)--> OsbZIP86 -->(decreases transcriptional activation)--> OsNCED3 (indirectly reducing ABA biosynthesis).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: microRNA regulating target mRNA by sequence-specific cleavage.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In ZH11 seedlings, miR2105 is repressed by ABA, drought (air exposure), and 150 mM NaCl treatments; its expression increases rapidly upon re-watering or salt recovery and shows an expression pattern opposite to OsbZIP86 across tissues and developmental stages.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA (0.5 μM) treatment, drought/water deficiency by air exposure or withdrawal of solution/irrigation, lacunar drought during reproductive phase, and 150 mM NaCl salt stress; exogenous ABA (2.5–10 μM) during seed germination.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: MicroRNA-mediated regulation of ABA biosynthesis, drought and salt stress tolerance, and stomatal movement in rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress: drought tolerance, salt tolerance, drought-induced ABA accumulation, stomatal movement, and grain yield under drought and salt.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After vegetative-stage drought and recovery, seedling survival was ~7% in ZH11, 15–18% in STTM2105, and 0–2% in miR2105-ox, crbzip86, and bZIP86-RNAi; under 4 h drought, leaf ABA contents were lower than ZH11 in miR2105-ox and higher in STTM2105; under ABA treatment, miR2105-ox seedlings showed longer shoots and roots than ZH11, indicating reduced ABA sensitivity.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: miR2105-ox, crbzip86, and bZIP86-RNAi lines exhibited reduced grain weight per panicle, seed setting, and thousand-grain weight under drought and salt treatments, whereas STTM2105 lines maintained agronomic traits similar to ZH11 under drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa L. cv. Zhonghua 11 (ZH11) and derived transgenic lines overexpressing osa-miR2105 (miR2105-ox) or expressing STTM2105 for miR2105 knockdown; rice protoplasts prepared from ZH11 and miR2105-ox; seeds and seedlings grown in Yoshida solution, soil boxes, and controlled field plots.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: miRNA sequencing (previously to identify miR2105), construction of 35S-driven miR2105 overexpression and Ubi-1-driven STTM2105 vectors, Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of ZH11, RT-qPCR of miRNA and mRNA, 5'-RLM-RACE for target cleavage mapping, drought and salt stress treatments in hydroponic culture and soil boxes, ABA treatments of seedlings and seeds, measurement of seedling survival, water loss rates, stomatal aperture by SEM, ABA content by ELISA, and agronomic trait analysis under normal and lacunar drought conditions.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation of osa-miR2105 overexpression (miR2105-ox) and short tandem target mimic knockdown (STTM2105) transgenic rice in ZH11 background; RT-qPCR of miR2105 and OsbZIP86 under ABA, drought and salt; 5'-RLM-RACE mapping of the miR2105-guided cleavage site in OsbZIP86 mRNA; drought and salt tolerance assays at vegetative and reproductive stages; measurements of water loss, stomatal aperture, ABA content, and ABA sensitivity in seed germination and seedling growth.
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: STTM2105 and miR2105-ox lines were tested in soil boxes and controlled field plots under heading-stage drought and lacunar drought to evaluate survival, seed setting, grain weight per panicle, and thousand-grain weight.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: osa-miR2105 was previously identified from rice by miRNA sequencing; in this study its target OsbZIP86 was predicted using psRNATarget and validated by expression analysis in miR2105-ox and STTM2105 lines and 5'-RLM-RACE mapping of the cleavage site.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Downregulation of miR2105 (STTM2105) increases drought and salt tolerance, enhances drought‑induced ABA accumulation, and improves grain yield under stress without obvious penalty on growth and major agronomic traits under normal conditions, making miR2105 a promising target for breeding stress-tolerant rice by weakening its negative regulation on OsbZIP86–OsNCED3–mediated ABA biosynthesis.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsbZIP86 is a homolog of wheat EmBP-1 and belongs to the rice bZIP family. It localizes exclusively to the nucleus in rice cells and N. benthamiana leaf epidermal cells. A mutated OsbZIP86 version with alanine substitutions at Ser149, Ser152, and Ser155 (OsbZIP86S149A/S152A/S155A) shows greatly reduced ability, especially when combined with OsSAPK10S177A, to activate OsNCED3pro:LUC, indicating these Ser residues are important phosphorylation sites for OsSAPK10-mediated activation.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsbZIP86 is a drought-, salt-, and ABA-inducible nuclear bZIP transcription factor whose expression is inversely regulated by miR2105.
2. Gain- and loss-of-function analyses show that OsbZIP86 positively regulates drought and salt tolerance, reduces water loss, promotes stomatal closure, and enhances drought-induced ABA accumulation, with negligible effects on growth under normal conditions.
3. OsbZIP86 directly binds G-box motifs in the OsNCED3 promoter and activates its transcription, especially under drought and ABA treatments.
4. OsbZIP86 physically interacts with and is phosphorylated by OsSAPK10, and this phosphorylation enhances its ability to activate OsNCED3 transcription, placing OsbZIP86 at the center of a miR2105–OsSAPK10–OsbZIP86–OsNCED3 module controlling ABA biosynthesis under drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A nuclear bZIP transcription factor that positively regulates drought- and salt-induced ABA biosynthesis and stress tolerance by directly binding to and activating the OsNCED3 promoter; overexpression enhances drought and salt tolerance and stomatal closure, whereas RNAi or CRISPR knockout reduces these traits under stress, with little effect under normal growth.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsbZIP86 directly binds G-box motifs in the OsNCED3 promoter and functions as a transcriptional activator; its transcriptional activity is enhanced by phosphorylation at Ser149/152/155 by the SnRK2 kinase OsSAPK10, which increases OsNCED3 promoter activity under drought and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in an ABA biosynthesis and signaling pathway under drought and salt, functioning downstream of miR2105 and OsSAPK10 and upstream of OsNCED3 to regulate drought-induced ABA production and stomatal closure.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ABA biosynthesis (indirectly promotes 9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase OsNCED3 transcription in the ABA biosynthetic pathway).
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: miR2105 --(miRNA-directed mRNA cleavage inhibition)--> OsbZIP86; OsSAPK10 --(protein–protein phosphorylation activation)--> OsbZIP86; OsbZIP86 -->(transcriptional activation)--> OsNCED3.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basic region-leucine zipper (bZIP) transcription factor; contains predicted serine phosphorylation sites in its regulatory region.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, shown by OsbZIP86-GFP co-localization with nuclear markers in rice protoplasts, co-localization with DAPI-stained nuclei in N. benthamiana leaf cells, and nuclear GFP signal in roots of Ubi:OsbZIP86-GFP transgenic rice.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsSAPK4, OsSAPK6, OsSAPK7, and OsSAPK10 (interaction shown by LCI in N. benthamiana); OsSAPK10 interaction further confirmed by GST pull-down in vitro, and by BiFC and Co-IP in vivo.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsbZIP86 expression is induced by ABA, drought, and salt treatments in ZH11 seedlings and cannot be upregulated by these stresses in miR2105-ox lines; it is mainly expressed in stem, leaf sheath, young leaf, and seed with lower expression in panicle; promoter–GUS staining confirms high expression in young leaf, leaf sheath, stem, and seed. Its temporal and spatial expression pattern is opposite to that of miR2105.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA (0.5 or 50 μM), drought/water deficiency by air exposure or withdrawal of solution/irrigation, lacunar drought from flowering to maturity, and 150 mM NaCl salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Transcriptional regulation of ABA biosynthesis, drought and salt tolerance, and stomatal movement via a bZIP transcription factor in rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress: drought tolerance, salt tolerance, drought-induced ABA accumulation, stomatal movement, and grain yield stability under drought and salt.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After vegetative-stage drought, survival of bZIP86-ox seedlings was ~15–18% versus ~7% in ZH11, whereas crbzip86 and bZIP86-RNAi seedlings had ~0–2% survival; bZIP86-ox leaves showed slower water loss than ZH11, while crbzip86 and bZIP86-RNAi had faster loss; under 4 h drought, ABA content in bZIP86-ox leaves was higher than in ZH11, while crbzip86 and bZIP86-RNAi had lower ABA content.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under drought and salt, OsbZIP86 overexpression plants maintain grain yield components similar to ZH11, whereas crbzip86 and bZIP86-RNAi lines show reduced grain weight per panicle, seed setting, and thousand-grain weight; OsbZIP86 also influences seed germination and vigor under ABA treatment through regulation of endosperm development- and seed vigor-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa L. cv. Zhonghua 11 (ZH11) wild type; transgenic lines overexpressing OsbZIP86 (bZIP86-ox, including OsbZIP86-GFP and OsbZIP86-6HA-GFP), OsbZIP86-RNAi lines, and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout mutants (crbzip86); OsbZIP86 promoter–GUS transgenic rice; rice protoplasts; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for transient assays; E. coli BL21 expressing GST-OsbZIP86 fusion protein.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation to generate overexpression, RNAi, CRISPR knockout, and promoter–GUS lines; RT-qPCR for expression analyses; drought and salt treatments in Yoshida solution and soil boxes; measurement of survival, water loss, stomatal aperture (SEM), ABA content (ELISA), and agronomic traits; GUS staining for promoter activity; subcellular localization by GFP fluorescence and DAPI staining; EMSA with G-box probes; ChIP-qPCR using anti-GFP to detect OsbZIP86 enrichment on OsNCED3 promoter fragments; dual-LUC reporter assays in N. benthamiana; LCI assays for interaction with OsSAPKs; GST pull-down; BiFC; Co-IP; in vitro phosphorylation with phos-tag SDS-PAGE; site-directed mutagenesis of Ser149/152/155.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Generation of Ubi:OsbZIP86-GFP overexpression (bZIP86-ox), OsbZIP86-RNAi, and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout (crbzip86) rice in ZH11 background; drought and salt stress assays at vegetative and reproductive stages; measurements of water loss, stomatal aperture, grain yield traits, ABA content, and ABA sensitivity; subcellular localization of OsbZIP86-GFP in rice protoplasts, N. benthamiana leaves, and roots; EMSA and ChIP-qPCR to show direct binding to G-box motifs in the OsNCED3 promoter; dual-LUC assays to show transcriptional activation of OsNCED3; LCI, pull-down, BiFC, Co-IP, and phos-tag SDS-PAGE to demonstrate interaction with and phosphorylation by OsSAPK10; mutagenesis of predicted phosphorylation sites to test effects on OsNCED3 promoter activation.
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: OsbZIP86 overexpression, RNAi, and CRISPR mutants were tested under heading-stage drought and lacunar drought in soil boxes and controlled field plots to evaluate survival, yield components, and grain traits under stress versus normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: OsbZIP86 (previously osZIP-1a) was an annotated bZIP transcription factor; in this study it was identified as a miR2105 target from 13 predicted targets using psRNATarget based on inverse expression changes in miR2105-ox and STTM2105 lines and validated by 5'-RLM-RACE and EST analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Constitutive overexpression of OsbZIP86 (bZIP86-ox) enhances drought and salt tolerance, decreases water loss, promotes stomatal closure, and increases drought-induced ABA accumulation without evident negative effects on growth and major agronomic traits under normal conditions, suggesting OsbZIP86 as a breeding target to improve stress tolerance by upregulating OsNCED3-dependent ABA biosynthesis only under stress.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsSAPK10 contains an autophosphorylation site at Ser177; mutation to Ala (OsSAPK10S177A) reduces the ability of the OsSAPK10–OsbZIP86 complex to activate OsNCED3pro:LUC, indicating the importance of OsSAPK10 autophosphorylation for full activation of OsbZIP86.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsSAPK10 was identified among SAPK family members as a strong interactor of OsbZIP86 that markedly enhances OsNCED3 promoter activity when co-expressed with OsbZIP86.
2. OsSAPK10 physically interacts with OsbZIP86 in vitro and in vivo, as shown by pull-down, BiFC, Co-IP, and LCI assays.
3. OsSAPK10 phosphorylates OsbZIP86 in vitro in an ATP-dependent manner, causing a phos-tag mobility shift that is reversed by alkaline phosphatase.
4. Autophosphorylation of OsSAPK10 at Ser177 and the presence of Ser149/152/155 in OsbZIP86 are important for maximal activation of OsNCED3pro:LUC, indicating that OsSAPK10-mediated phosphorylation activates OsbZIP86 to promote OsNCED3 transcription.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A SnRK2 family stress/ABA-activated protein kinase that interacts with and phosphorylates OsbZIP86, thereby enhancing OsbZIP86-dependent activation of OsNCED3 transcription and promoting ABA biosynthesis under drought/ABA.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsSAPK10 interacts with OsbZIP86 and phosphorylates it on Ser149/152/155, producing a mobility shift in phos-tag SDS-PAGE that is abolished by alkaline phosphatase; phosphorylation enhances OsbZIP86’s transcriptional activation of the OsNCED3 promoter.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Component of ABA signaling under drought and salt stress; functions upstream of OsbZIP86 in the miR2105–OsSAPK10–OsbZIP86–OsNCED3 module that controls drought-induced ABA biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsSAPK10 --(protein–protein phosphorylation activation)--> OsbZIP86 -->(transcriptional activation)--> OsNCED3 (enhancing ABA biosynthesis under drought/ABA).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK2 family serine/threonine protein kinase (stress/ABA-activated protein kinase, SAPK).
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsbZIP86 (physical interaction supported by LCI, GST pull-down, BiFC, and Co-IP).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Although expression and activation of OsSAPK10 under drought, ABA, and NaCl were documented previously, this study assayed OsSAPK10 function mainly under conditions mimicking ABA/drought signaling in transient systems (dual-LUC in N. benthamiana).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: SnRK2 kinase regulation of bZIP transcription factors and ABA biosynthetic gene expression in drought and ABA signaling in rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress and hormone signaling: ABA signaling, drought- and salt-related signaling leading to ABA biosynthesis via OsbZIP86–OsNCED3.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Co-expression of OsSAPK10 with OsbZIP86 significantly increased OsNCED3pro:LUC activity compared with OsbZIP86 alone, whereas OsSAPK10S177A and OsbZIP86S149A/S152A/S155A reduced this enhancement (quantified as LUC/REN ratios in dual-LUC assays).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Previous studies reported that overexpression of OsSAPK10 reduced water loss in detached leaves and increased ABA sensitivity, consistent with OsbZIP86-related phenotypes, but these organismal phenotypes were not directly re-measured in this study.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsSAPK10 constructs fused to GFP (OsSAPK10-GFP), FLAG (SAPK10-FLAG), NLuc/CLuc fragments, and MBP tag; co-expression with OsbZIP86-FLAG or OsbZIP86-GFP in N. benthamiana leaves; rice protoplasts for BiFC; E. coli BL21 expressing MBP-OsSAPK10 fusion protein.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning of OsSAPK10 into MBP, GFP, FLAG, NLuc/CLuc, and BiFC vectors; LCI assays in N. benthamiana; GST pull-down with GST-OsbZIP86 and MBP-OsSAPK10; BiFC in rice protoplasts; Co-IP in N. benthamiana leaves using anti-GFP and anti-FLAG; in vitro phosphorylation in kinase buffer with and without ATP and alkaline phosphatase followed by phos-tag SDS-PAGE; dual-LUC reporter assays with OsNCED3pro:LUC and effectors OsSAPK10, OsSAPK10S177A, OsbZIP86, and OsbZIP86S149A/S152A/S155A.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: LCI assay in N. benthamiana leaves to identify interaction between OsbZIP86 and OsSAPKs; GST pull-down to show direct interaction between MBP-OsSAPK10 and GST-OsbZIP86 in vitro; BiFC in rice protoplasts and Co-IP in N. benthamiana to confirm OsbZIP86–OsSAPK10 interaction in vivo; in vitro kinase assay with phos-tag SDS-PAGE demonstrating OsSAPK10-dependent phosphorylation of GST-OsbZIP86; dual-LUC reporter assays showing that co-expression of OsSAPK10 with OsbZIP86 strongly enhances OsNCED3pro:LUC activity, and that OsSAPK10S177A and OsbZIP86S149A/S152A/S155A mutations reduce this activation.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: OsSAPK10 is an annotated member of the rice SnRK2 (SAPK1–SAPK10) family; in this study it was selected as a candidate bZIP86-interacting kinase from OsSAPK1–10 using LCI screening in N. benthamiana, and OsSAPK10 showed the strongest enhancement of OsNCED3 promoter activity when co-expressed with OsbZIP86.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a stress/ABA-activated SnRK2 kinase that phosphorylates and activates OsbZIP86 to enhance OsNCED3 expression, OsSAPK10 is part of a module promoting drought-induced ABA biosynthesis; although direct breeding evaluation was not performed here, modulation of OsSAPK10 activity together with OsbZIP86 could be used to fine-tune ABA signaling and drought tolerance in rice.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Among the five rice OsNCED genes, OsNCED3 shows the strongest drought-induced upregulation in OsbZIP86-ox plants, whereas OsNCED5 is downregulated and OsABA8ox2 is upregulated under drought, suggesting OsNCED3 is the primary target of OsbZIP86 in ABA biosynthesis with possible feedback regulation of other ABA metabolic genes.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsNCED3, a key ABA biosynthetic gene, contains multiple G-box motifs in its promoter.
2. OsbZIP86 directly binds the P1 G-box region of the OsNCED3 promoter in vitro (EMSA) and in vivo (ChIP-qPCR), with stronger enrichment under drought and ABA.
3. OsbZIP86 overexpression or miR2105 knockdown cause strong upregulation of OsNCED3 under drought, whereas OsbZIP86 knockdown/knockout or miR2105 overexpression reduce OsNCED3 expression.
4. Dual-LUC assays show that OsbZIP86 activates the OsNCED3 promoter and that this activation is further enhanced by OsSAPK10, linking OsNCED3 expression to the miR2105–OsSAPK10–OsbZIP86 regulatory module.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key ABA biosynthetic gene whose expression is directly activated by OsbZIP86 binding to its promoter; OsNCED3 transcript levels are strongly upregulated in OsbZIP86-ox and STTM2105 and downregulated in miR2105-ox, crbzip86, and bZIP86-RNAi under drought, correlating with ABA content and drought tolerance of these lines.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsbZIP86 binds specifically to a G-box-containing P1 region located 387 bp upstream of the OsNCED3 ATG and activates OsNCED3 transcription; OsbZIP86 occupancy on the OsNCED3 promoter is enhanced under drought and ABA, and co-expression with OsSAPK10 further increases OsNCED3 promoter-driven LUC activity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions as the main ABA biosynthetic target gene in the miR2105–OsSAPK10–OsbZIP86–OsNCED3 module that mediates drought-induced ABA accumulation and stomatal closure in rice.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ABA biosynthesis: 9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase (NCED) step that cleaves violaxanthin and neoxanthin to form xanthoxin, the rate-limiting step in de novo ABA biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsbZIP86 -->(transcriptional activation)--> OsNCED3; OsSAPK10 -->(via phosphorylation of OsbZIP86)--> OsNCED3 (enhanced transcription); miR2105 -->(indirect repression via OsbZIP86 cleavage)--> OsNCED3.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsbZIP86 (binds to G-box elements in the OsNCED3 promoter to regulate its transcription).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under normal conditions, OsNCED3 shows only slight upregulation in OsbZIP86-ox and STTM2105; under drought, OsNCED3 transcripts are substantially increased in OsbZIP86-ox and STTM2105 and decreased in miR2105-ox, crbzip86, and bZIP86-RNAi compared with ZH11.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought (water deficiency), ABA treatment, and salt (NaCl), which modulate OsbZIP86 activity and thereby OsNCED3 expression; OsNCED3 expression was assayed mainly under drought and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Transcriptional control of ABA biosynthetic gene expression and its role in drought-induced ABA accumulation in rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress and hormone metabolism: ABA biosynthesis under drought and salt, influencing ABA accumulation, stomatal movement, and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought, OsNCED3 transcript levels are substantially increased in OsbZIP86-ox and STTM2105 and significantly decreased in miR2105-ox, crbzip86, and bZIP86-RNAi compared with ZH11, consistent with higher or lower ABA contents, respectively (quantified by RT-qPCR and ABA ELISA).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Changes in OsNCED3 expression correlate with changes in ABA content and drought tolerance across miR2105 and OsbZIP86 transgenic lines, affecting stomatal closure and water loss under drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: ZH11 wild type; OsbZIP86 overexpression, RNAi, and CRISPR lines; miR2105-ox and STTM2105 lines; OsbZIP86-6HA-GFP transgenic rice for ChIP; N. benthamiana leaves for dual-LUC reporter assays; E. coli expressing GST-OsbZIP86 protein for EMSA.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Promoter analysis to identify G-box motifs in the 2-kb OsNCED3 promoter; RT-qPCR of OsNCED3 in different transgenic backgrounds; EMSA with biotin-labeled G-box probes and competition assays; ChIP-qPCR using anti-GFP in OsbZIP86-6HA-GFP lines under normal, drought, and ABA treatments; dual-LUC reporter assays with OsNCED3pro:LUC co-expressed with OsbZIP86 and OsSAPKs in N. benthamiana; ABA content measurements in leaves of transgenic lines under drought.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR analysis of OsNCED3 expression in OsbZIP86 and miR2105 transgenic lines under normal and drought conditions; EMSA using G-box-containing probes from the OsNCED3 promoter and GST-OsbZIP86 to show specific binding; ChIP-qPCR with OsbZIP86-6HA-GFP lines to demonstrate in vivo enrichment of OsbZIP86 on G-box-containing OsNCED3 promoter regions, enhanced by drought and ABA; dual-LUC assays showing that OsbZIP86 activates OsNCED3pro:LUC and that this activation is further enhanced by OsSAPK10.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: OsNCED3 is an annotated NCED family member previously identified as a major drought-inducible ABA biosynthetic gene in rice; in this study, its promoter G-box motifs were analyzed and OsbZIP86 was tested and validated as an upstream transcriptional regulator.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsNCED3 encodes a key ABA biosynthetic enzyme and is strongly induced by drought via OsbZIP86; however, previous work cited here indicates that direct overexpression of ABA biosynthetic genes such as OsNCED3 can improve stress tolerance but negatively affect leaf traits and yield, suggesting that for breeding it is preferable to modulate OsNCED3 through upstream regulators (miR2105 and OsbZIP86) to enhance drought tolerance without growth penalties.
